textByHash.ba46a5ea97a4f26d513734b667aa4bb524586feb: That door leads to the training ground for the Knights of the Round Table.
textByHash.5d5a360e68e8b075e81e6656dff6dedf82d32fcb: Of course, my lord. But I must tell you the rules first. Would you like to hear them now?
textByHash.14ed79998981446f3ec07d05828762adb2ed8f03: The training ground is a melee combat area only. Merlin has cast a spell over the grounds to prevent anyone using Prayer, Ranged or Magic.
textByHash.1cfde20f6f51d45447595f6e35a7291a644ee399: Upon entering the grounds, you will fight each of the Round Table Knights in turn. Each knight is more skilled than the last, but all have different attack styles that can be exploited. There are eight knights in all, and
textByHash.ceb10173426ed04ea6d467f7c946c8dd4794b5bf: each will do damage not only to your hitpoints, but also your combat stats. If you should die in the grounds, Merlin will teleport you back here. You will not lose any of your items.
textByHash.40d70b518779907e484334c99809645c6c56ca53: If you feel you cannot complete the battle, you may exit the training grounds. However, if you exit or die, you will have to start over with the first knight the next time you enter.
textByHash.ff2469bb58d6e123b1ca039462eb019aa225fb2a: If you are a great warrior and defeat Sir Lancelot, mightiest of all knights, you will receive great rewards. Two new prayers will become available to you and your respawn point will be set to Camelot, as well as 20,000
textByHash.0b86bd5d16bb6312497e263fcba9ffc0be7569e5: XP in to each of your combat stats.
textByHash.a97f97f84e8f6133fe10fdd27da50f3607d02f07: If you do not want your respawn set to Camelot, Merlin can change it for you.
textByHash.6d5ee86f2afbc93005c48a53d05e9ae183c8d92e: Would you like me to repeat any of that for you?
textByHash.ef050cf9e3e1bad02cd94ed07d222172a713920b: Only melee combat is allowed within the training grounds. Merlin will prevent the use of Prayer, Ranged and Magic.
textByHash.c2e89f10e34e0dcdd5f19cb8a643db06420d5d6e: These grounds are for only the best knights of the land. It is not just testing your combat ability, but your honour and your chivalry. You must face your enemy head on, honourably and bravely.
textByHash.8094f5cf334862cc5acd4ae636180790f252fffa: Be watchful of your stats, as the Knights can do damage to your combat stats as well as your hitpoints.
textByHash.c40758f22ecb09e2391a2021fc93e4dea37762a2: As these are only training grounds, we keep our knights safe. If you should die, you will be teleported back to here and all of your items will be safe.
textByHash.ff155480918bb19c893859e628cd41d007e7d420: However, leaving the training grounds, in anyway, will mean you will have to start from the beginning the next time you enter. These grounds do not favour cowards.
textByHash.04f606a8761c69165e59f0162fd0bf76e069f37c: You fight each knight of the Round Table, starting with Sir Bedivere and finishing with Sir Lancelot. Leaving means you will have to begin fighting Sir Bedivere again and work your way back up.
textByHash.a3f0748447a292d1f07ff99f50681b9dcd1b5a48: If you log out during a fight, upon logging back in you will fight the knight you were fighting when you logged out.
textByHash.c670538788bd00048843397f9084e1013ecb1457: King Arthur believes in rewarding his knights for their chivalry. Having proven yourself in combat against his knights, and in your services rendered to Camelot, you will be rewarded with two new prayers, and a new
textByHash.adedbbbf934f88efd2862803291108749c125eb3: respawn at Camelot, as well as 20,000 XP in to each of your combat stats.
textByHash.9093fe4cba3666a20a45afd6199ac77669145c1a: Ah, patience is a virtue in knights, my lord. Fight in the training ground and earn your reward to find out!
textByHash.5931255389f18b1f7ea774b399615280e12618a5: Ah, Merlin has offered, as a reward for your help, to let your respawn in the safety of Camelot, should you befall misfortune elsewhere in the world. Should you wish to change your respawn back to Lumbridge, simply
textByHash.e262c802a98822984f05d7d702874bce42a7bae5: Good day, my lord. Is there anything I can do for you?
textByHash.022957a5046312eaaa01feafbe2a76c6ea7cb8cf: Hello. I am the squire to Sir Vyvin.
textByHash.0224e23521e965fa96f4fd8c295a3257abe0c9a4: Well, Sir Vyvin is a good guy to work for, however, I'm in a spot of trouble today. I've gone and lost Sir Vyvin's sword!
textByHash.d5f54e08aa6f93b79f8de1fecaf0c5165031f911: Well now, if I knew THAT it wouldn't be lost, now would it?
textByHash.e6de42673ae5b7214501f5109b575d09dd612e3d: No. I was carrying it for him all the way from where he had it stored in Lumbridge. It must have slipped from my pack during the trip, and you know what people are like these days...
textByHash.b668970024e864765470fcca50f62209e46fe521: Someone will have just picked it up and kept it for themselves.
textByHash.2a82b5098392c1dec5c83529ceb2ffdb838f4082: Yes. You can get bronze swords anywhere. But THIS isn't any old sword.
textByHash.2a1bd976aba22f403b60f8f004cd765aa9ab0f83: The thing is, this sword is a family heirloom. It has been passed down through Vyvin's family for five generations! It was originally made by the Imcando dwarves, who were
textByHash.4d6f623188983b22fdffbb29efffd06fa5d68ca5: a particularly skilled tribe of dwarven smiths. I doubt anyone could make it in the style they do.
textByHash.7bd46e482497869e05a4260f7e025df2d6f7c52b: Thanks for the offer. I'd be surprised if you could though.
textByHash.4d1e6b331673049e2f3ef5bfab1e9223ca6f3a58: I'm not a hundred percent sure the Imcando tribe exists anymore. I should think Reldo, the palace librarian in Varrock, will know; he has done a lot of research on the races of Gielinor.
textByHash.79d7ccbbbedaa27fe72e11f3af3c9bfe30bf0909: I don't suppose you could try and track down the Imcando dwarves for me? I've got so much work to do...
textByHash.591f3689771e7ec52844d336df23ae2260fb4b85: Thank you very much! As I say, the best place to start should be with Reldo...
textByHash.8a6f0c88a58c6b1a09b4734c52aedea3bad3ac5c: What, really? That is wonderful to hear! Where is he then?
textByHash.233d751d053bc14dd655d023c0f2d8aa595e944c: Oh man... I'm in such trouble...
textByHash.8a1e234d557b7472be8db8f55ce60a12bca8f6c1: Yes, me too. I'm not looking forward to telling Vyvin I've lost it. He's going to want it for the parade next week as well.
textByHash.220ab3e2faf2dfb6d4f3b7baf01d6926ffc5ce85: He doesn't know yet. I was hoping I could think of something to do before he does find out, But I find myself at a loss.
textByHash.516af603545fa07ab818627b7de1adc9ac3890da: No, sorry, I'm loyal to Sir Vyvin.
textByHash.3b846b466493eee2ad6ef67086ebcbb9310d0eb1: So how are you doing getting a sword?
textByHash.1baf9cc242e9022d6453b40ee2974328e8982189: Please try and find them quickly... I am scared Sir Vyvin will find out!
textByHash.a1e395a1caafd0948294a39dbdee077e8c000225: A picture eh? Hmmm.... The only one I can think of is in a small portrait of Sir Vyvin's father... Sir Vyvin keeps it in a cupboard in his room I think.
textByHash.af8957c387b6f963f937b2274d095ee7f308ff55: Please don't let him catch you! He MUSTN'T know what happened!
textByHash.fec7bdb7da372d07502f728ed62a7993ee8a2c2d: Please try and get it quickly... I am scared Sir Vyvin will find out!
textByHash.696668b57bab92230558699e56e1b5c0bde144c0: So can you un-equip it and hand it over to me now please?
textByHash.4efa6566fb14301aebf0a81dddd6b7c7820076f0: Thank you, thank you, thank you! I was seriously worried I would have to own up to Sir Vyvin!
textByHash.6308edb7ba3d0c83c8bb27f20447997a5c24393d: I'm sorry, my lord, but you cannot enter this area.
textByHash.3498e5101a712ef26ea7fccc68170941acf2785d: Congratulations on succeeding in the Knight Waves, my lord.
textByHash.357e61c24561bd468594c47e3e65eea59bf29b47: I'm afraid not, my lord. Once you have won, you cannot re-enter.
textByHash.3248adba2b7f08d1afa4edebe05e0174a318c9ee: Sorry, but I'm busy right now.
textByHash.90aaec96f10159d49c3037f4908256cd74c5e996: Sorry, but I have lots of work to do.
textByHash.d5be914f38e453bfb2274bcf851a3f2a3199546c: Not too bad, but the work is definitely tiring.
textByHash.eaec929b203fcb43087f4929ed0ffe1bbed4d735: Well I could do with a good rest, but fine otherwise.
textByHash.51dec85006fb0e898d6b37839bb84ca91f6bb884: I'm Vicks, squire to Sir Culler Flife. He's away on a surveillance mission to Burthorpe, so he sent me down here to do some cleaning.
textByHash.7491ef594fec479696c523609b12b904f6801f61: Don't tell the knights, but I'm just not in the mood for it! I've served for years, and worked really hard, but they're not even thinking of making me a knight.
textByHash.e4143ec82447106cce73c0e6d458ad4e31228639: I don't know what the problem is. At my last review, I heard the examiner muttering something about my name, but I've no idea what he meant by that.
textByHash.04f91abab566775311608d11e340d0056bd04a10: Keeping an eye on those Imperial Guard. We can't trust 'em, you know. But I shouldn't talk about his mission.
textByHash.de29c44867ca6fa330890a05fbc66c9cecaa6741: This place? It's the castle crypt. The greatest White Knights are buried here, after a lifetime in the service of Saradomin. At least, that's how it used to be.
textByHash.ff88c50cb4faefffefb2bc5880dfc0308003f421: When I was a new recruit, they brought me here and told me that if I served Saradomin faithfully, and with distinction, maybe I'd earn the honour of burial in this place too.
textByHash.03a567edf67ec12ae5e19c5a964b365472ffaf5b: But if you think about it, that's never going to happen - there just isn't space for more tombs! Anyway, my plan is to avoid dying, and maybe marry one of the barmaids when I retire.
textByHash.a6fbe875379a3e37755b1c3436aa85900a92ae37: It's because of something the Temple Knights have, called the Gaze of Saradomin. They get the option of being brought to Falador when they die, so Death has to maintain a presence in the area.
textByHash.b2463ca5c50a9d39919e3d23fa5e8e7e2a09493c: That portal takes you to Death's own realm. He's got portals in all the places where people go when they die - Lumbridge, Edgeville and the rest. I hate it - the thing's terrifying!
textByHash.27dd376bbe3073bb4e222915d16971d2d0262d29: Keeping an eye on those Imperial Guard, sir. We can't trust 'em, you know. But I shouldn't talk about his mission, even with a knight such as yourself.
textByHash.43df138fc8c2209370016ad4baffb45db0a0bcb7: Keeping an eye on those Imperial Guard, madam. We can't trust 'em, you know. But I shouldn't talk about his mission, even with a knight such as yourself.
textByHash.4407f5a4aac0b14617ecfbd7ac7cc1f68732505a: It's because of the Gaze of Saradomin. You Temple Knights get the option of being brought to Falador when you die, so Death has to maintain a presence in the area.
textByHash.3479f77e6b31f186cdd63275e2e0e0f9c444ff84: Hi, how can I help you?
textByHash.20a8f80b142ba3e45f469f6d284caafda42c1f55: I'm a Squire for the Void Knights.
textByHash.e46d5a7acdfcd15f6773e00f5defaef2ca6e0919: The Void Knights, they are great warriors of balance who do Guthix's work here in Gielinor.
textByHash.b47e810e70922df211e163b7c313ac3b9b911bce: Entry is strictly invite only, however we do need help continuing Guthix's work.
textByHash.ba066b54da2ef718124035982238f5f93098380b: Ah well you see we try to keep Gielinor as Guthix intended, it's very challenging. Actually we've been having some problems recently, maybe you could help us?
textByHash.86c0a141b81ff0f63213c509555e8395210d1591: Well the order has become quite diminished over the years, it's a very long process to learn the skills of a Void Knight. Recently there have been breaches into our realm from somewhere else, and strange creatures
textByHash.7d0f3cf38670b023d90d71c537a321fca13fa55b: have been pouring through. We can't let that happen, and we'd be very grateful if you'd help us.
textByHash.65913eb508ef4d2969ad573b840fcbfe9e769574: We send launchers from our outpost to the nearby islands. If you go and wait in the lander there that'd really help.
textByHash.fb842d1aba3845872d358d9d9a2b028ff6d80bec: It is the name that Guthix gave to this world, so we honour him with its use.
textByHash.5b39b0d2b365092e282cfe76b0f8badcfe7e0b08: To the Void Knight outpost. It's a small island just off Karamja.
textByHash.6789ac2526beda061ffdb724dcb182b548260219: Ok, but please come back soon and help us.
textByHash.d0efb30595fbfbe5536604d956824e0d2ca0ffee: This is our outpost. From here we send launches out to the nearby islands to beat back the invaders.
textByHash.46c194097882af36fdb68422eb03bbfd697c65ae: Recently there have been breaches into our realm from somewhere else, and strange creatures have been pouring through. We can't let that happen, and we'd be very grateful if you'd help us.
textByHash.692bb70f8f9f40d774994831a734cd378ac25491: Sorry, you'll need to speak to one of the Knights about that. I'm just a humble squire with years of training still to undergo!
textByHash.83cf5ffc0d49a32402f9c06b33b2cc6b41c72191: You must defend the Void Knight while the portals are unsummoned. The ritual takes twenty minutes though, so you can help out by destroying them yourselves! Now GO GO GO!
textByHash.74a614c7f2e215928974e7228a2c836dd0c2f564: This island is being invaded by outsiders and the Void Knight over there is using a ritual to unsummon their portals. We must defend the Void Knight at all costs, however if you get an opening you can destroy the
textByHash.a2e63bd3ffdb4b840fe1aef18c0b174ca8962c54: There are trees on the island. You'll need to chop them down for logs and use a hammer to repair the defences. Be careful though, the trees here don't grow back very fast so your resources are limited!
textByHash.ce169775422668aada15734f52e53c6c075be59d: Away you go then, the lander will take you back.
textByHash.ea6821d1487b721daefd7e33631a9bf6bddc4b6d: Of course I'll repair it, though the materials may cost you. Just hand me the item and I'll have a look.
textByHash.aa7272f4349c1269aa7c705db41ef1e3135be5e3: There you go, happy doing business with you!
textByHash.cc990211737a6648083f577d5863adb0a1106035: Sorry friend, but I can't do anything with that.
textByHash.8a37fce3ad93b5d5ae832c83ec2f7927a1d19bf4: Hello friend! Many thanks for all of your help! Vyvin never even realised it was a different sword, and I still have my job!
textByHash.ab512efa8593ad55a8507f8574a0fc65125bbe0b: I have a question for you.
textByHash.2ba3fa01f7b19ac0b94e945a7cc6928e5bc24315: Please enter the answer to the question.
